documentProcessing.heroDescription: Turn PDFs, scans, and forms into structured records with reasoning traces — so agents can act on the data, not just read it.
bulletPoints: Extract fields, line items, and nested tables from statements, reports, and compliance documents.
Every output includes confidence scores, field lineage, and evidence traces.
Exception routing keeps agents moving — only edge cases reach human review.

features.heading: Structured extraction that agents trust.
features.description: Schema control, table fidelity, and evidence traces — every output is validated before it reaches a downstream agent.
features.fieldTableExtractionDesc: Single fields, repeated sections, nested tables, and multi-page structures — all typed and schema-validated.
features.confidenceScoredDesc: Every extracted value carries a confidence score, field lineage, and review state. Agents know what to trust.
features.agentHandoffDesc: Pass validated records into reconciliation, underwriting, or compliance agents with clear schemas and evidence traces.
features.pipelineHeading: From raw document to trusted structured record.
features.pipelineDescription: A lattice-style system view keeps the page aligned with the existing use-case architecture while introducing the document extraction workflow shown in the reference.

ingestion.heading: Standardize document intake across every source.
ingestion.description: Pull from inboxes, scanners, portals, and shared drives into one pipeline. Every file is parsed, validated, and routed before agents see it.
ingestion.supports: Supports PDF, PNG, HTML, email attachments, and scanned images.
ingestion.noChanges: No changes to existing operational tooling required.

classification.heading: Classify first. Extract with precision.
classification.description: Identify document types, segment pages, and route each section to the right extraction model — before extraction begins.
classification.signalModelDesc: Combine content, layout, and metadata cues for reliable routing.
classification.opsControlDesc: Map categories to queues, workflows, or agent actions with clear review gates.
classification.taxonomyNote: Build custom document taxonomies for finance, operations, claims, or compliance-heavy workflows without sacrificing auditability.

table.heading: Capture every row, column, and nested structure.
table.description: Extract tabular data from financial statements, rent rolls, and operational reports — then pass structured records to the data mapping layer for semantic indexing.
table.tableFidelityDesc: Preserve rows, columns, and key-value relationships across noisy layouts.
table.agentHandoffDesc: Pass normalized outputs into review queues, ERP flows, or rules engines.
table.designNote: Designed for finance-heavy workflows where tables need to be trusted, compared, and routed without manual spreadsheet cleanup.

dataMapping.heading: Agents are only as reliable as the data map they retrieve.
dataMapping.description: Traditional RAG achieves 25-30% accuracy on financial questions. Our agentic data maps achieve 99%. The difference: structured entity graphs, field-level lineage, and retrieval-ready indexes that agents can navigate with precision — not guess from raw embeddings.
dataMapping.dataMapNote1: The data map connects entities, fields, tables, and evidence locations — so agents operate on structured records, not fragmented document text.
dataMapping.dataMapNote2: This is the layer that separates keyword-matching from financial-grade retrieval: every query resolves to the correct entity, time period, and source coordinate.
dataMapping.kgRagDesc: Multi-hop reasoning over knowledge graphs — high construction cost, limited scalability
dataMapping.nl2sqlDesc: Structured queries against relational databases — schema-dependent, structured data only
dataMapping.agenticSearchDesc: Multi-agent verification + iterative refinement — production-grade financial accuracy
dataMapping.reasoningDesc: Agents traverse explicit semantic relationships instead of inferring structure from raw text. Entity graphs connect issuers to accounts to transactions — no guesswork.
dataMapping.retrievalDesc: Route each query to the exact field, table, and entity — with evidence traces. Traditional RAG hits 25-30% on financial questions. Agentic data maps reach 99%.
dataMapping.calculationDesc: Anchor numerical operations to normalized, typed values with source lineage. Comparisons, ratios, and totals stay reliable because every number is traceable.

humanReview.heading: Human review where it matters. Automation everywhere else.
humanReview.subheading: Confidence thresholds determine what flows and what stops.
humanReview.description: Flagged fields get annotation. Resolved fields flow downstream. Every correction feeds back into the extraction model — so the exception queue shrinks over time.
humanReview.exceptionQueueDesc: Escalate only low-confidence fields, ambiguous entities, or missing evidence.
humanReview.auditTrailDesc: Keep reviewer actions, approvals, and corrections tied to the source record.
humanReview.reviewerControlsDesc: Approve, reject, or correct extracted values with source context preserved.
humanReview.confidenceRoutingDesc: Send only edge cases to humans while trusted fields continue automatically.

cta.heading: Built for regulated, data-heavy industries.
cta.seeItWork: See it work on your documents.

videoAudio.subtitle: 8-dimension multimodal annotation for video generation models.
videoAudio.heroTitle: Train Video Generation Models With Real Multimodal Data.
videoAudio.heroDescription: From raw footage to model-ready training data—we annotate the 8 dimensions that Seedance, Sora, and Veo actually need: scene semantics, object tracking, physical motion, camera grammar, dialogue, music, and sound effects.
videoAudio.playingAnnotation: Playing annotation timeline with synchronized tracks
videoAudio.dimensionStackDesc: Scene, tracking, motion, camera, and audio layers synchronized to frame-level precision.
videoAudio.hours100kDesc: Automated MLLM pipelines scale to 100K+ hours with consistent quality.
videoAudio.frameLevelPrecisionDesc: 33ms temporal precision at 30fps. Phoneme-level alignment for dialogue.
videoAudio.modelCompatibilityDesc: Direct input to Seedance, Sora, Veo, Kling, Wan training pipelines.

capabilities.heading: Scene Understanding Annotation: The Foundation for Video Generation.
capabilities.description: State-of-the-art video generation models don't train on isolated labels. They need 8 annotation dimensions working together—scene content, narrative arcs, object tracking, physical motion, camera grammar, dialogue, music, and sound effects—all synchronized to frame-level precision.
capabilities.foundationNote1: Scene understanding annotation is the semantic foundation. It classifies what's happening, who's involved, and why—so every other dimension has context.
capabilities.foundationNote2: The remaining 7 dimensions build on that foundation: tracking maintains identity, motion captures physics, camera encodes cinematography, and audio layers synchronize dialogue, music, and sound effects.

items.title: Scene Content & Semantics
items.description: Dense scene descriptions: subjects, actions, environment, lighting, atmosphere, art style. The semantic foundation that all other dimensions reference.
items.detail: Scene type · narrative beat · actor roles · event boundaries

items.title: Narrative & Storytelling
items.description: Emotional progression, dramatic tension, story beats, character motivations. Enables models to generate coherent storytelling—not just random motion.
items.detail: Three-act structure · emotional valence · narrative function

items.title: Object & Character Tracking
items.description: Multi-object identity persistence across occlusions and scene changes. Character consistency labels maintained across shots with pose estimation.
items.detail: Persistent IDs · cross-scene re-ID · keypoint tracking

items.title: Physical Motion Dynamics
items.description: Physics-aware motion labels: gravity, momentum, collision, material deformation. Annotates whether motion obeys real-world physical laws.
items.detail: Physics scoring · collision detection · material properties

items.title: Camera Grammar & Cinematography
items.description: Professional cinematography labels: shot types, camera movements (dolly, pan, crane), transitions, depth of field, composition rules.
items.detail: Shot types · camera movements · transitions · composition

items.title: Dialogue & Speech
items.description: Phoneme-level lip-sync alignment, speaker identification, emotion labels, multilingual/dialect support. Frame-precise word-to-mouth mapping.
items.detail: Lip-sync · speaker ID · emotion · multilingual support

items.title: Music & Score
items.description: Beat markers, tempo detection, genre classification, mood labels. Enables models to generate video that moves in rhythm with music.
items.detail: Beat markers · tempo · genre · mood · key progression

items.title: Sound Effects & Ambient
items.description: Causal action-sound correspondence: 'glass shatters because it hit the floor.' Material properties, spatial positioning, room tone.
items.detail: Action-sound mapping · material labels · spatial audio

sceneUnderstanding.heading: The Foundation Layer: What's Happening, Where, and Why.
sceneUnderstanding.description1: Before tracking objects or annotating audio, we establish the scene foundation: What type of scene is this? What's the narrative intent? Who are the actors? What actions are unfolding?
sceneUnderstanding.description2: This semantic layer anchors all other annotations. Every tracking ID, motion label, camera movement, and audio cue references this foundation.
sceneUnderstanding.liveSceneParsingDesc: Timecoded multimodal understanding with linked evidence

items.title: Scene classification & intent
items.description: Classify scene type (urban crossing, indoor cafe, wuxia fight), narrative beat (setup, climax, resolution), and actor roles—the semantic anchor for all other dimensions.
items.metric: Scene type · narrative beat · actor roles · event boundaries

items.title: Cross-modal evidence linking
items.description: Link dialogue to speaker, sound effects to visual events, and camera movement to narrative intent—so every annotation references the scene foundation.
items.metric: Speaker links · action-sound pairs · camera-narrative mapping

trackingSegmentation.heading: From Scene Semantics to Object-Level Precision.
trackingSegmentation.description1: Once the scene foundation is established, we track individual entities across time. Persistent IDs maintain character consistency. Segmentation masks capture precise boundaries.
trackingSegmentation.description2: Both dimensions reference the scene understanding layer for context—tracking IDs map to scene actors, segmentation masks align with scene geometry.

items.title: Object & Character Tracking
items.description: Multi-object identity persistence across occlusions and scene changes. Persistent IDs maintain character consistency. Pose estimation and keypoint tracking capture movement. All tracking references the scene understanding foundation for context.
items.metric: Persistent IDs · cross-scene re-ID · pose estimation · keypoint tracking

items.title: Instance Segmentation
items.description: Pixel-precise object boundaries frame-by-frame. Material property labels for physics modeling. Occlusion handling maintains mask quality. Segmentation references scene geometry and spatial relationships from the foundation layer.
items.metric: Instance masks · material properties · occlusion handling · scene geometry

annotationShowcase.heading: The Full Multimodal Stack: Motion, Camera, and Audio Layers.
annotationShowcase.description: Beyond scene semantics and object tracking, we annotate the dimensions that make video generation cinematic: physical motion dynamics, camera grammar, dialogue, music, and sound effects—all synchronized to frame-level precision.

items.title: Physical Motion Dynamics
items.description: Gravity, momentum, collision, material deformation—annotated with physics scoring per frame. Models learn what physically plausible motion looks like.
items.metric: Physics score · collision events · material properties

items.title: Camera Grammar & Cinematography
items.description: Shot types (close-up, wide, medium), camera movements (dolly, pan, crane, Hitchcock zoom), transitions, and composition rules—the vocabulary of cinematic generation.
items.metric: Shot types · camera movements · transitions · composition

items.title: Dialogue & Music
items.description: Phoneme-level lip-sync with SyncNet validation (offset ≤3 frames). Beat markers and tempo detection for rhythm-aligned generation. Speaker ID and emotion labels across multilingual content.
items.metric: Lip-sync · beat markers · speaker ID · emotion · multilingual

items.title: Sound Effects & Ambient
items.description: Causal action-sound correspondence: blade clash at frame 78, mud splash at frame 82. Material property labels, spatial positioning, and room tone for physically plausible Foley audio.
items.metric: Action-sound pairs · material labels · spatial audio · room tone

cta.heading: Model-Ready Training Data for Video Generation.
cta.description: Scene semantics, persistent object tracking, physical motion, camera grammar, and multi-track audio—annotated at frame-level precision and structured for Seedance, Sora, and Veo training pipelines.

items.title: Model-Assisted Video Labeling
items.description: Turn raw scenes into machine-readable supervision with temporal tags, object tracks, shot boundaries, and synchronized audio cues.
items.label: Auto timeline

items.title: Active Learning for Multimodal Review
items.description: Prioritize uncertain frames, refine labels faster, and keep video, audio, and event annotations aligned in one review loop.
items.label: Human in loop

items.title: Foundation Models for Scene Understanding
items.description: Use multimodal foundation models to accelerate segmentation, tracking, speech alignment, and event extraction across long-form video.
items.label: Scene intelligence

syntheticData.breadcrumb: Synthetic Training Data for Agentic LLMs
syntheticData.heroTitle: Train LLMs to Reason, Plan, and Act With Synthetic Data.
syntheticData.heroDescription: Generate structured training datasets for fine-tuning, RLHF, and evaluation. We synthesize reasoning traces, tool-calling trajectories, code execution paths, and mathematical proofs—the training signals that transform base LLMs into capable agents.
syntheticData.trainingDataSubtitle: Structured datasets for fine-tuning and RLHF.
syntheticData.trainingDataDescription: We generate the multi-step reasoning traces, execution-aware code samples, and tool-calling trajectories that LLMs need to learn agentic behaviors. Every dataset includes verification, critique loops, and failure recovery paths.
syntheticData.trainingPipelineDesc: Reasoning, code, math, and tool-calling datasets for fine-tuning and RLHF.
syntheticData.reasoningDesc: Multi-step CoT with critique loops and evidence graphs for fine-tuning.
syntheticData.codingDesc: Execution-verified samples with tests, patches, and error recovery paths.
syntheticData.mathsDesc: Symbolic proofs with checkpoints and formal verification for RLHF.
syntheticData.toolCallingDesc: Schema-safe actions with argument validation and state tracking.
syntheticData.synthesisEngineValue: Generate reasoning traces, code paths, tool sequences
syntheticData.verificationLayerValue: Execution checks, formal proofs, multi-model consensus
syntheticData.trainingOutputValue: Fine-tuning datasets, RLHF preferences, evaluation benchmarks
syntheticData.samples100kDesc: Scale to millions of training examples with automated synthesis pipelines.
syntheticData.executionVerifiedDesc: Code runs, math checks, tool calls validate against schemas.
syntheticData.rlhfReadyDesc: Preference pairs for reasoning quality, safety, and alignment.

benefits.heading: Why Synthetic Data for Agent Training?
benefits.scalabilityDesc: Generate millions of training examples without human annotation. Automated pipelines scale from 1K to 100K+ samples with consistent quality.
benefits.verifiabilityDesc: Every code sample executes, every math proof validates, every tool call checks against schemas. Execution-based filtering ensures training data is correct, not hallucinated.
benefits.diversityDesc: Evol-Instruct and difficulty adaptation create training curricula from simple to complex. Clustering-based diversity metrics prevent overfitting on narrow distributions.
benefits.platformHeading: The Training Data Platform for Agentic LLMs.
benefits.seedPromptsHeading: From seed prompts to verified training datasets.
benefits.seedPromptsDescription: We handle the full synthesis pipeline: seed prompts → evolve variations → execute verification → export training datasets. Fine-tune on reasoning traces, run RLHF on tool-calling preferences, evaluate on execution-verified benchmarks.

fabrication.heading: Generate Training Data From First Principles.
fabrication.description: We synthesize four core data modalities — each with domain-specific verification — so every sample an agent trains on is structurally sound.

items.title: Reasoning Traces
items.description: Multi-step chain-of-thought with critique loops and evidence graphs. Methods: Self-Instruct, STaR, Process Reward Models.

items.title: Code Execution
items.description: Execution-aware samples with tests, patches, and error recovery. Methods: OSS-Instruct, Evol-Instruct, Genetic Instruct.

items.title: Math Proofs
items.description: Symbolic steps with checkpoints and formal verification. Methods: MetaMath, NuminaMath, theorem proving.

items.title: Tool Calling
items.description: Schema-safe actions with argument validation and state tracking. Methods: APIGen, ToolACE, environment simulation.

impact.heading: Synthetic Data That Improves Model Capabilities.
impact.description: Training on synthetic data isn't just augmentation—it's how modern LLMs learn to reason, code, and use tools. Our datasets are designed for the training loops that matter: supervised fine-tuning, RLHF, and continuous evaluation.

stats.label: Diverse training samples per capability domain

impact.caseStudy: See how DeepSeek, Qwen, and Seed use synthetic data to train specialized agents.

pipeline.heading: Ideate, Generate, Verify.
pipeline.description: Multi-agent pipelines — Ideator agents brainstorm edge cases, Generator agents synthesize trajectories, Evaluator agents verify correctness. The result: diverse, execution-verified training data at scale.
pipeline.calloutText: Turn prompts, policies, and source records into machine-readable training assets.
pipeline.ctaHeading: Find the training data pipeline that fits your fine-tuning workflow.

scale.heading: Scale From 1K Seed Examples to 100K Training Samples.

items.title: Curriculum learning: start simple, evolve to complex.
items.description: Bidirectional evolution ensures coverage from trivial to expert-level tasks. Dynamic difficulty scaling based on model's current solvability. Prevents plateauing on simple tasks while maintaining learning curve.

items.title: Code runs, math validates, tool calls check schemas.
items.description: Every code sample executes in sandboxes, every math proof validates against formal systems, every tool call checks API schemas. Multi-model consensus for subjective tasks. Rejection sampling filters hallucinations.

items.title: Clustering-based metrics ensure semantic coverage.
items.description: Evol-Instruct generates variations: harder, easier, different constraints. Clustering-based diversity metrics prevent overfitting. Covers wide semantic area across problem space to avoid model collapse.

suite.heading: Pre-built training datasets for common agentic capabilities.
suite.description: Custom synthesis for your domain: legal reasoning, medical coding, financial tool use. Integrate with your fine-tuning infrastructure: HuggingFace, Weights & Biases, Vertex AI.

items.title: Reasoning Datasets
items.description: Multi-step CoT traces for GSM8K, MATH, ARC, HellaSwag. Includes critique loops, evidence graphs, and verification steps. Format: JSON-L with reasoning chains and final answers.

items.title: Code Execution Datasets
items.description: Execution-aware samples for HumanEval, MBPP, SWE-Bench. Includes patches, tests, error recovery, and repo-level context. Format: Parquet with code, tests, execution logs.

items.title: Tool-Calling Datasets
items.description: Schema-safe trajectories for API calling, web navigation, CLI tools. Includes intent mapping, argument validation, state tracking. Format: JSON-L with tool schemas and execution traces.
